narrative: US-Iran tentative 60-day MOU introduces a geopolitical risk discount as gold firms +1.22% near $4,536; equities +0.56% and DXY -0.22% retreat while VIX 15.8 remains normal. Fed's Musalem and Williams maintain hawkish vigilance on inflation, anchoring the 300bp USD/JPY carry at 159.20 despite a moderate -1.11 put skew in 25d RR. NZD/JPY bilateral G8 divergence (1.15% spread) persists as NZD leads peers (+0.77% avg). Watch for any RBNZ policy shift following the May 27 meeting cycle.
